meaning: n.杠杆的支点,支点,叶附属物
examples: The decision is the strategic fulcrum of the Budget.
Iran is the fulcrum of an unstable region.
We unconsciously assume the center of a picture corresponds to a fulcrum.
In the future I reckon China will be the fulcrum for natural resource shares.
Gerrard was at the fulcrum of the home side's overwhelming dominance, with Torres the chief outlet.
